title: Week 3 Day 4 (1/2) — Models & quantization
summary: Part 1 of 2 · `AutoModelForCausalLM` · 4-bit NF4 · load pattern · model zoo.

sections.heading: Low-level Hugging Face
points: **Causal LM** = autoregressive next-token model (the usual chat LLM).
PyTorch code in `model` object — `print(model)` shows layers.
Lab: `week3/day4.ipynb` · accept Meta license for Llama on Hub.

sections.heading: Quantization
sections.remember: **Quantization** stores weights in fewer bits (e.g. **4-bit NF4**) → less VRAM, small quality drop — better than deleting most weights.
points: 16/32-bit weights → 4-bit ≈ ~4× memory savings; math is simpler on GPU.
**NF4** maps 4 bits to a floating-point range (not just integers 0–15).

sections.heading: BitsAndBytesConfig
sections.remember: Pass **`quantization_config`** into `from_pretrained` — model downloads full weights then loads quantized into RAM.
sections.code:
from transformers import BitsAndBytesConfig

quant_config = BitsAndBytesConfig(
    load_in_4bit=True,
    bnb_4bit_quant_type="nf4",
    bnb_4bit_use_double_quant=True,
)

model = AutoModelForCausalLM.from_pretrained(
    model_id,
    quantization_config=quant_config,
    device_map="auto",
)

sections.heading: Standard inference pattern
sections.remember: Almost every open-source chat run: **pad_token = eos_token** → **apply_chat_template** → **`.to("cuda")`** → **generate**.
points: `tokenizer.pad_token = tokenizer.eos_token` — avoids padding errors on many models.

sections.heading: Create model + tensors
sections.remember: `inputs` is a dict (e.g. `input_ids`) on GPU — **`model.generate(**inputs)`** runs the forward loop.
points: Small Llama 3.2 ~1B + 4-bit may use ~1GB VRAM after load.
First run downloads from Hub (slow); rerun is faster.
No Llama access? Skip Llama cells; run Phi/Gemma/Qwen.
